body:
This PR contains: 

- A few improvements that will make https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/pull/4848 easier.
- A couple of fixes for edge cases.
- Include `dimension_type` to `NetCDFWriter` which controls the precision type for dimensions, with a default value of `Float64`.
- Perhaps most importantly, it brings the treatment of arrays by the `NetCDFWriter` closer to what Oceananigans does. In particular singleton dimensions are kept if the location has a well-defined value (i.e. the value is not `nothing`, which NetCDF can't handle). This means will remove singleton dimensions _only_ when:
  - the location on that dimension is nothing (e.g. a reduced field)
  - the topology in that dimension is `Flat`

 To- do list:

- [x] Should we enforce that spatial dimension is always Float64 to avoid alignment issues? We already do this with time for the same reason (see https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/3056).
- [x] Special treatment for free surfaces
- [x] Do not squeeze singleton dimensions in slices
- [x] Add test for when the grid passed to `NetCDFWriter` is different from `model.grid`
- [x] Add test to make sure that singleton dimensions aren't improperly squeezed/removed
title: Improvements and fixes to `NetCDFWriter`

labels.description: Pull requests that update a dependency file
labels.name: dependencies

title: Add Brenner et al. (2025) paper to documentation

body:
This isn't complete, but like #4979 this is a beginning for more extensive linting checks.  The tests introduced here should pass locally for me (so in principle this PR is good to go as is), but I haven't checked I haven't broken anything in the process, I guess CI will tell us 🙂

I know the PR looks a bit intrusive, but there's really lots of cruft in the code, these checks (together with #4979 and future related PRs) are aimed at making Oceananigans more maintainable in the long run.

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪
